name: Hai Ba Trung
description: The Trung sisters, Trung Trac and Trung Nhi, are national heroines of Vietnam who rebelled against Chinese rule in 40 AD. Born in Jiaozhi (northern Vietnam) during the Eastern Han dynasty, they were daughters of a wealthy Lac Lord. Trung Trac's husband, Thi Sach, was killed by the Chinese administrator To Dinh, sparking the sisters' rebellion. Trung Trac, the elder sister, led the uprising, quickly gaining support from other Lac Lords and the Vietnamese people, who were resentful of Chinese oppression and heavy taxation. Their forces captured 65 citadels and drove out To Dinh, establishing Trung Trac as queen (Trung Vuong). They ruled independently for three years, attempting to restore Vietnamese traditions and culture. However, the Han dynasty sent General Ma Yuan with a large army to quell the rebellion. Despite fierce resistance, the Trung sisters were eventually defeated in 43 AD. According to legend, rather than surrender, they drowned themselves in the Hat River. Their bravery and leadership in the face of foreign domination have made them enduring symbols of Vietnamese patriotism and resistance. They are revered as goddesses and their story is a cornerstone of Vietnamese national identity, symbolizing the spirit of independence and the strength of Vietnamese women.
subtitle: Vietnamese Heroines and Rebel Queens
achievements: Leading a successful rebellion against Chinese rule
Becoming symbols of Vietnamese national identity
Inspiring future generations of Vietnamese revolutionaries
Demonstrating the strength and leadership of Vietnamese women

keyMyths.title: The Rebellion's Spark
keyMyths.description: The rebellion was ignited by the oppressive rule of To Dinh and the execution of Thi Sach, Trung Trac's husband. This act fueled the sisters' determination to liberate their people and avenge Thi Sach's death.

keyMyths.title: The Queens' Rule
keyMyths.description: For three years, the Trung sisters ruled Vietnam, attempting to restore Vietnamese culture and traditions. Their reign symbolized a brief period of independence and self-determination for the Vietnamese people.

keyMyths.title: Suicide at Hat River
keyMyths.description: Faced with inevitable defeat against Ma Yuan's forces, the Trung sisters chose to drown themselves in the Hat River rather than surrender. This act cemented their status as martyrs and symbols of Vietnamese resistance.

sources.author: Keith Weller Taylor
sources.description: A comprehensive historical account of the early history of Vietnam, including the Trung sisters' rebellion.
sources.text: A comprehensive historical account of the early history of Vietnam, including the Trung sisters' rebellion.
